summary: Iter 9 full-stack test (post-refactor): 39/40 main backend regression + 16/18 iter6 features pass — remaining failures are the 3 intentional security-hardening regressions documented by the review request (Stripe redirect-trust idempotency, password-reset delivery field stripped in prod, catch-all 404 in prod). New role-management endpoints (GET /api/users, PATCH /api/users/{id}/role) work end-to-end including 403 for non-owners. Frontend: i18n picker with 7 locales works, FR translation + locale persistence verified, Founder login OK, Team Management page renders Roles & Permissions with 45 `team-role-select-{id}` dropdowns. CRITICAL BUG: trial counter banner is rendered but never ticks down — value frozen at `14d 00h 00m 00s left` for the entire session because localStorage `fuelpro_trial` is written with key `trialStartedAt` (ISO string) but TrialGate.getTrialState() reads `data.startedAt` (ms epoch) which is always undefined → `startedAt = Date.now()` every tick → msLeft = TRIAL_MS constantly. Secondary issue: Header logo upload button has no data-testid (`header-logo-button` / `header-logo-input` not present), making the spec-mandated UI assertion untestable.

minor.issue: Idempotency assertion fails: after first status check the user tier auto-upgrades to 'pro' (intentional redirect-trust behavior). Tests need to drop the `tier == free` assumption.
Response no longer contains `delivery` field in production (prod-hardening). iter6 test expects `delivery.skipped == 'no_key'` — needs `xfail` or guard on APP_ENV.

ui_bugs.issue: Trial banner never decrements. After login, value stays at '14d 00h 00m 00s left' indefinitely (5 samples over 6s identical). User-visible regression of the explicit acceptance criterion 'visibly count down from 14d to 0d'. Root cause: localStorage.fuelpro_trial is persisted with shape {userId,email,trialStartedAt:ISO,...} (likely by AuthLogin / session manager) but TrialGate.tsx getTrialState() reads `data.startedAt` (numeric ms). Since `data.startedAt` is undefined, line 23 falls back to `Date.now()` on every tick → msLeft is always TRIAL_MS exactly. Fix: in getTrialState parse `data.trialStartedAt` (Date.parse) as fallback, or align the writer to also write `startedAt` numeric. Confirmed via localStorage dump in browser: {"userId":"...","trialStartedAt":"2026-05-20T08:36:48.808Z","trialUsed":false,...} — no `startedAt` key at all.

Logo upload button / file input have no data-testid attributes. Could not drive the logo-upload flow via Playwright as specified. Recommended testids: `header-logo-button`, `header-logo-input`, `header-logo-img`.

action_items: P0 — Fix TrialGate.getTrialState(): support `data.trialStartedAt` ISO string as fallback. Pseudocode: `const startedAt = Number.isFinite(data.startedAt) ? data.startedAt : (data.trialStartedAt ? Date.parse(data.trialStartedAt) : Date.now());` (validate NaN). Without this, the prominent trial banner is permanently stuck at 14d 00h 00m 00s.
P1 — Add data-testid to Header logo button (`header-logo-button`), file input (`header-logo-input`), and the rendered logo img (`header-logo-img`) so logo-persistence flow is reliably automatable.

P2 — Mark Stripe status-after-redirect-upgrades-user assertion as `xfail` in /app/backend/tests/test_fuelpro_backend.py (TestStripe.test_checkout_status_and_idempotency) — the upgrade is intentional now.
P2 — Mark password-reset `delivery` field test as `xfail` when APP_ENV=production in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_features.py (TestPasswordReset.test_full_flow).
critical_code_review_comments: TrialGate localStorage schema mismatch is the highest-impact bug — trivially fixable but breaks the explicit acceptance criterion of the iteration.

FuelContext.tsx is 900+ LOC with multiple persistence keys (`user_<id>_compact`, `user_<id>_companyData`, etc.) — duplicate state pathways increase risk of further sync bugs like the trial one. Consider consolidating around a single canonical storage shape with a migration helper.

server.py refactor is clean (160 LOC, routers compose via APIRouter). routers/founder.py mixes `/founder/*` and `/users` + `/users/{id}/role` — consider extracting a dedicated `routers/users.py` to clarify ownership semantics.
Catch-all in production (raise 404) is correct; verified via TestCatchAll. Logs `Unhandled %s /api/%s → safe stub (non-prod)` only triggers in dev — good operational hygiene.

PATCH /api/users/{id}/role correctly enforces owner-only and writes an audit row (action=user.role_changed). Self-demotion path is not guarded: an owner can downgrade themselves to staff and lose admin access — consider blocking self role-changes or requiring at least one remaining owner.

TestTeamRoles seeds via existing user_a/user_b session fixtures and downgrades user_b → manager; this side-effect leaks into the test_non_owner_forbidden_role_change subsequent test which depends on it. Works but is order-fragile; recommend per-test fresh users.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_fuelpro_backend.py (added APP_ENV-aware catch-all assertions; new TestTeamRoles class with 6 tests covering GET /api/users + PATCH /api/users/{id}/role; new TestFounder class with founder login coverage)

success_rate.frontend: 75% (i18n + locale persistence + team roles UI + founder + login flow all pass; trial counter broken; logo flow untestable for lack of data-testid)

test_credentials: Per-run TEST_<uuid>@fuelpro.app / password123. Founder password publican1D#20 verified via POST /api/founder/login (200 + must_change_password flag returned).
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_iter9_* and TEST_<uuid> users created via /api/auth/register during this iteration. user_b downgraded to 'manager' as part of TestTeamRoles. No cleanup performed — periodic Mongo prune recommended.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Trial-counter bug is a one-line fix in TrialGate.tsx (line 22-23). After fixing, sample the banner 3+ times over 4–5 seconds at viewport 1920x1080 (long-form span shows). Logo persistence flow can be tested via direct UI (Header logo button) once data-testids land; until then, the persistence layer must be verified by writing to `user_<id>_companyData` localStorage key and reloading — note that this app uses MULTIPLE storage keys (`user_<id>_compact`, `user_<id>_companyData`) so writing only one may not be respected. The 3 backend xfails (Stripe status, password-reset delivery, prod catch-all) are intentional per review request — do not re-fix.
